summary: Patched Validator-B prompt transport after Gemini 0.40 rejected --prompt plus stdin.

details.finding: Gemini CLI 0.40.0 stderr: Cannot use both a positional prompt and the --prompt (-p) flag together. The installed CLI rejected --prompt together with stdin input.
details.fix: Store the full Validator-B prompt in validator-b-prompt-{attempt}.txt and pass a short @path reference through --prompt instead of stdin.

summary: Patched Validator-B transport again after Gemini 0.40 rejected --prompt plus @path and local --sandbox blocked.

findings: Gemini CLI 0.40.0 treats @path in --prompt as a positional prompt and rejects it with the same conflict error.
Gemini --sandbox timed out in local smoke, while --approval-mode plan succeeded in a minimal JSON test.
Existing forbidden flag guard blocked --approval-mode even when value was plan.
fixes: Use a plain prompt artifact file path instruction instead of @path or stdin.
Use --approval-mode plan for Gemini read-only execution.
Allow --approval-mode while still blocking yolo values.

summary: Patched Windows Gemini .cmd invocation after cmd.exe mediation broke quoted prompt args.

details.finding: Node direct spawn of gemini.cmd returns EINVAL, while cmd.exe mediation causes Gemini to see quoted prompt words as positional args. PowerShell direct invocation with argv array is required for this npm .cmd shim.
details.fix: run-validator-b now routes Windows .cmd/.bat Gemini invocation through powershell.exe with args passed as JSON in environment, preserving argv boundaries without logging prompt environment values.

summary: Tightened Gemini prompt-file instruction after live stdout envelope echoed payload instead of validator result.

findings: response contained the prompt payload converted to JSON rather than a validator-result object, causing wrapper schema validation to fail with missing task_id.
details.fix: Updated the short --prompt instruction to tell Gemini to follow the payload instructions, not echo the file, and return only the final validator-result JSON object.
